To download CBSE Class 12 question papers, you can use the official CBSE website or authorized educational platforms[1][2]. Please note that there is no separate category for "improvement exam" papers; the improvement exam follows the same pattern and syllabus as the main board examination[3].

### Official CBSE Sources
You can download past board examination papers directly from the official CBSE website:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to [cbse.gov.in](https://www.cbse.gov.in)[3].
2.  **Navigate to Question Papers:** Look for the **"Academic"** or **"Board Examinations"** section[1][4].
3.  **Access Archives:** CBSE provides an archive of "Previous Years' Question Papers" where you can select the relevant year and class (XII) to download PDFs for various subjects[5][6][7].

### How to Prepare for the Improvement Exam
*   **Use Standard Board Papers:** Since the improvement exam is essentially a retake of the board exam, practicing the regular board question papers from previous years is the most effective way to prepare[3][8].
*   **Sample Papers & Marking Schemes:** On the same CBSE academic portal, you can also download **Sample Question Papers (SQP)** and **Marking Schemes**[1][6]. These are excellent resources for understanding the expected format and how to structure your answers for higher marks[2][9].
*   **Educational Platforms:** Websites such as Vedantu, Oswaal360, and others also host collections of CBSE Class 12 previous year papers with solutions, which can be useful for self-assessment and revision[2][10][11].

**Important Note:** If you are preparing for the improvement exam, ensure you are using the syllabus relevant to the current academic year, as curricula can sometimes be updated[1][12]. You can verify the latest syllabus on the CBSE Academic website under the "Curriculum" section[1][4].

### **Official Sources for Download**
*   **CBSE Academic Website:** This is the primary source for all official study materials[2].
    *   **Sample Question Papers & Marking Schemes:** Visit the [CBSE Academic SQP page](https://cbseacademic.nic.in/) to find the latest Sample Question Papers and their corresponding Marking Schemes for various academic years[1][3][2].
    *   **Question Banks:** You can also access official Question Banks and "Additional Practice Questions" designed to help you prepare for the board exams[4][5][2].
*   **Previous Years' Question Papers:** For practice with real exam papers from past years, you can visit the official [CBSE Board Examination page](https://www.cbse.gov.in/) or the respective archives on the academic portal[6][7][8].

It is highly likely you are referring to the **AISSCE** (All India Senior School Certificate Examination), which is the formal name for the **CBSE Class 12 Board Examinations**[1][2][3]. 

Alternatively, you might be thinking of the **Cambridge AICE Diploma** (Advanced International Certificate of Education), which is a separate international qualification and not part of the standard CBSE board curriculum[4].

### For CBSE Class 12 (AISSCE) Sample Papers:
If you are looking for official practice materials for your CBSE Class 12 board exams, you can access them directly through the official CBSE academic website:

*   **Official Website:** Visit [cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in) to find the **Sample Question Papers (SQP)** and **Marking Schemes (MS)** for the current academic year[5][6].
*   **Resources Available:** The board provides subject-wise sample papers and marking schemes to help you understand the exam pattern, question format, and grading criteria[7][8].
*   **How to Access:** Navigate to the "Sample Question Paper" section on the CBSE Academic website, where you can download PDFs for subjects across Science, Commerce, and Humanities streams[5][6][8].

If you were specifically looking for the Cambridge AICE Diploma materials, please visit the official Cambridge International Education website.

To access question papers for the CBSE compartment (now officially referred to as **supplementary**) examinations, you can visit the official CBSE website's dedicated portal[1][2].

### Accessing Question Papers
*   **Official Website:** Navigate to [cbse.gov.in/cbsenew/question-paper.html](https://www.cbse.gov.in/cbsenew/question-paper.html)[1].
*   **Portal Navigation:** On this page, you will find categorized links for "Previous Years' Question Papers"[1][3]. Look for the sections specifically labeled **"Question Papers for Compartment Examination"**[4][1].
*   **Available Materials:** The portal provides PDF downloads for various subjects across both Class X and Class XII[4][1][5].

### Exam Structure and Pattern
If you are preparing for the supplementary/compartment examination, please note the following regarding the structure:

*   **Identical Format:** The supplementary examination follows the **exact same pattern, syllabus, and marking scheme** as the main board examination[6][7][8].
*   **Difficulty Level:** The difficulty level is designed to be comparable to the main board exam[6][8].
*   **Syllabus:** There is no reduction in the syllabus; you are expected to prepare the full curriculum as prescribed for the academic year[8].
*   **Paper Composition:** Question papers typically include a mix of Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs), short-answer, and long-answer questions, consistent with the standard board paper design[9].
*   **Practical/Internal Assessment:** For most subjects, your internal assessment or practical marks from the original main examination are carried forward[10][8]. The supplementary exam primarily tests you on the theory component[10].
